records.ety: [L. erodere, erosum; e out + rodere to gnaw. See Rodent.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Eroded; p. pr. & vb. n. Eroding.]

defs: To eat into or away; to corrode; as, canker erodes the flesh.
quotes: The smaller charge is more apt to . . . erode the gun.

defs: To wear away; as, streams and glaciers erode the land.
To produce by erosion, or wearing away; as, glaciers erode U-shaped valleys.
to reduce or lessen as if by eroding; as, a politician's base of support is eroded by evidence of corruption; the buying power of the dollar is eroded by inflation.
